supply_chain.dependencies: Five suppliers (Apple, Samsung, HP, Sony, LG) represent approximately 55% of total merchandise purchased. Complex supply chain heavily reliant on vendor imports from China (approximately 55% of products purchased) and Mexico (approximately 20% of products purchased). Consumer electronics dependent on rare earth elements predominantly processed in China. Exclusive brand products manufactured by contract manufacturers in China, Mexico and Southeast Asia.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions from geopolitical tensions, tariffs, trade route disruptions (Red Sea attacks, Panama Canal, Suez Canal), natural disasters, pandemics, labor strikes, port delays. Concentration risk with top suppliers. Vendor import dependencies from China and Mexico. Single-source dependencies for rare earth elements from China. Semiconductor and television panel sourcing risks from Taiwan. Limited long-term written contracts with vendors.

regulatory_risks: Tariffs on products and parts imported from China and Mexico
Trade policy changes and renegotiation of trade agreements
Data privacy and security regulations (HIPAA, GDPR, California Consumer Privacy Act, state data privacy laws)
FDA regulations for health sector offerings
CMS and State Medicaid Agencies regulations
Environmental legislation on energy transition, greenhouse gas emissions, electronics recycling, water, product materials

Labor and employment laws (union organization rules, independent contractor classification, diversity and inclusion laws, minimum wage, sick time, paid leave, non-compete covenants, scheduling requirements)
Potential federal ban on arbitration clauses in consumer or employee contracts
Tax law changes (U.S., state, foreign jurisdictions)
Corporate governance and public disclosure regulations (SEC, NYSE, FASB)
Mobile communications and electronic device certification regulations

sanction_risks: Trade restrictions and tariffs involving China and Mexico
Potential disruption from Taiwan-China tensions affecting semiconductor and TV panel manufacturing
Impact from ongoing conflicts in Ukraine and Middle East on fuel prices, inflation, global supply chain, cybersecurity
Red Sea cargo ship attacks disrupting global trade flows
South China Sea tensions affecting product manufacturing and distribution
International trade route disruptions (Panama Canal, Suez Canal)
